data.title: Identity and Vitality
data.summary: The Sun represents the central organizing principle of the personality: identity, vitality, will, creative force and conscious direction.
dos_json: interpret as the core identity function
connect it to vitality and direction
consider sign, house and aspects before judging expression
donts_json: do not reduce it to ego only
do not assume automatic confidence
do not interpret it without chart context
prompt_hints_json: Describe the Sun as the conscious center.
Explain how the person seeks to express vitality and identity.
Avoid simplistic zodiac-sign clichés.
data.micro_note: The Sun shows the principle of conscious selfhood, not the whole personality.

data.title: Emotion and Inner Security
data.summary: The Moon represents emotional life, instinctive reactions, memory, habits, needs and the search for inner safety.
dos_json: interpret as emotional and instinctive function
include needs and safety
consider family and habit patterns
donts_json: do not treat the Moon as weakness
do not over-pathologize sensitivity
do not ignore bodily and emotional rhythms
prompt_hints_json: Describe how the person seeks comfort and emotional safety.
Mention instinctive reactions.
Use soft and embodied language.
data.micro_note: The Moon shows how life is felt from the inside.

data.title: Thought and Communication
data.summary: Mercury represents perception, thinking, language, learning, exchange, analysis and the way information is processed.
dos_json: interpret as the mental and communicative function
connect it to learning and exchange
adapt interpretation to sign and house
donts_json: do not reduce Mercury to intelligence
do not assume clarity without context
do not ignore nervous-system tone
prompt_hints_json: Describe how the person thinks, learns and communicates.
Mention both strengths and possible mental traps.
Keep language precise.
data.micro_note: Mercury shows how experience becomes thought, word and exchange.

data.title: Attraction and Value
data.summary: Venus represents attraction, pleasure, affection, harmony, values, beauty and the way one gives and receives appreciation.
dos_json: interpret as value and attraction function
include pleasure and relational style
connect beauty to values
donts_json: do not reduce Venus to romance only
do not make it automatically passive
do not ignore self-worth
prompt_hints_json: Describe what the person values and attracts.
Mention love, pleasure and aesthetic sensitivity.
Balance sweetness with shadow patterns.
data.micro_note: Venus describes what feels desirable, valuable and harmonious.

data.title: Action and Desire
data.summary: Mars represents action, assertion, desire, courage, anger, libido, competition and the way energy is directed toward goals.
dos_json: interpret as action and desire function
include courage and conflict
look at how Mars is directed
donts_json: do not make Mars only negative
do not ignore sexuality and anger
do not confuse assertiveness with aggression
prompt_hints_json: Describe how the person acts, wants and fights.
Mention both drive and reactivity.
Use active, concrete language.
data.micro_note: Mars shows how energy becomes action.

data.title: Expansion and Meaning
data.summary: Jupiter represents expansion, confidence, belief, wisdom, growth, opportunity, generosity and the search for meaning.
dos_json: interpret as expansion and meaning function
include both opportunity and excess
connect it to belief systems
donts_json: do not make Jupiter only lucky
do not ignore inflation
do not promise guaranteed success
prompt_hints_json: Describe where growth, confidence and meaning seek expression.
Mention opportunity with moderation.
Avoid deterministic luck language.
data.micro_note: Jupiter expands; whether that helps depends on context.

data.title: Structure and Responsibility
data.summary: Saturn represents structure, time, discipline, limits, responsibility, maturity, fear and the process of consolidation.
dos_json: interpret as structure and maturity function
include fear and mastery
connect difficulty to development
donts_json: do not make Saturn only negative
do not use fatalistic language
do not ignore its stabilizing power
prompt_hints_json: Describe what must be built, matured or disciplined.
Mention limits without fatalism.
Frame Saturn as demanding but constructive.
data.micro_note: Saturn restricts in order to form and mature.

data.title: Disruption and Liberation
data.summary: Uranus represents disruption, awakening, independence, rebellion, originality, innovation and sudden change.
dos_json: interpret as liberation and disruption function
include originality and instability
consider generational context
donts_json: do not make Uranus only chaos
do not ignore its innovative intelligence
do not over-personalize slow-planet effects
prompt_hints_json: Describe what seeks freedom or awakening.
Mention disruption as potentially creative.
Ground interpretation in house and aspects.
data.micro_note: Uranus breaks continuity to reveal new possibilities.

data.title: Dream and Dissolution
data.summary: Neptune represents imagination, spirituality, compassion, dreams, idealization, confusion, dissolution and transcendence.
conflict_patterns_json: losing self in fantasy or rescue
dos_json: interpret as imagination and dissolution function
include both inspiration and confusion
mention boundaries when relevant
donts_json: do not make Neptune purely spiritual
do not ignore deception or vagueness
do not present illusions as facts
prompt_hints_json: Describe where sensitivity, dreams or idealization operate.
Use poetic but grounded language.
Balance compassion with discernment.
data.micro_note: Neptune opens the invisible but can blur reality.

data.title: Transformation and Power
data.summary: Pluto represents transformation, depth, crisis, power, shadow, compulsion, regeneration and processes of death and rebirth.
dos_json: interpret as transformation and power function
handle intensity carefully
include regeneration, not only crisis
donts_json: do not use fear-based language
do not make Pluto only destructive
do not over-personalize generational placements
prompt_hints_json: Describe where deep transformation or power dynamics appear.
Use careful psychological language.
Balance shadow with regeneration.
data.micro_note: Pluto reveals what must die, transform or be reclaimed.
